verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II Golgi glycosyltransferase, but it has a well-documented dual-localization: a fraction reaches the plasma membrane where it functions as a cell-surface lectin/adhesion molecule, notably on sperm (binding ZP3) and in cell-cell/cell-matrix recognition. The longer isoform with extended cytoplasmic tail is preferentially trafficked to the PM. Surface pool is minor relative to Golgi but documented across multiple cell types.
